summary: Feature request to support SkiaSharp in Blazor Server (not just WASM) by marshaling SkiaSharp drawing commands over SignalR to a client-side renderer, similar to the BlazorExtensions/Canvas pattern.

reproEvidence.environmentDetails: Blazor Server and Blazor WebAssembly deployment models

repoLinks.description: Example of Blazor Extension pattern for HTML5 Canvas
mattleibow prototype using SKPicture serialization over SignalR
Sample Blazor WASM app using SkiaSharp

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: Blazor WASM support was added via PR #1811 and released in SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor, but server-side Blazor rendering remains unsupported. The JSModuleInterop still requires browser platform and throws NotSupportedException outside WASM.

analysis.summary: This is a feature request from 2020 to support SkiaSharp in Blazor Server mode, not just Blazor WebAssembly. The WASM-side was addressed by PR #1811 which added SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor, but server-side support (marshaling draw commands over SignalR) was never implemented. The Blazor views are decorated with [SupportedOSPlatform("browser")] and the JS interop layer explicitly requires in-process JS runtime, making server-side impossible. mattleibow prototyped an SKPicture-based serialization approach (BlazorSkiaSharp repo) that serializes drawing commands on the server and deserializes them on the WASM client, but this was never productized.
analysis.rationale: Classified as feature-request because it asks for a fundamentally new capability (server-side Blazor rendering) that does not exist and was never implemented. The WASM support that was added (PR #1811) addresses only half the original request. The issue has 19 reactions and 48 comments showing strong community interest. The architecture would require a new rendering pipeline that serializes draw commands from server to client.

keySignals.text: the feature request is once SkiaSharp can run in Client Blazor, please support SkiaSharp as a Blazor Extension
keySignals.interpretation: Core ask: dual deployment model support (client + server) via Blazor Extension pattern.

keySignals.text: I have effectively created a separate JS library to do all this work, and it coincidentally just uses .NET as the main driver
keySignals.interpretation: Maintainer built a prototype using SKPicture serialization for server-to-client rendering.

keySignals.text: SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor package still works in Blazor WebAssembly apps only
keySignals.interpretation: As of May 2022, the server-side part remains unimplemented and community is asking for a timeline.

keySignals.text: I have merged #1811 and will release a public preview of the new client-side, wasm SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor package
keySignals.interpretation: Only the WASM half of the request was delivered.

codeInvestigation.finding: Class is decorated with [SupportedOSPlatform("browser")] and the pre-NET7 path throws NotSupportedException if not IJSInProcessRuntime (WASM only). NET7+ path uses JSHost.ImportAsync which also requires browser platform. Server-side Blazor cannot use this.

SKCanvasView is marked [SupportedOSPlatform("browser")] — entirely WASM-specific. Rendering creates pixel buffers in managed memory and blits via JS interop to an HTML canvas, a pattern incompatible with server-side rendering.
SupportedPlatform is restricted to 'browser' only, confirming package is WASM-exclusive.

nextQuestions: Should SkiaSharp server-side Blazor render as static images (PNG/JPEG) streamed to the client, or use the SKPicture serialization approach mattleibow prototyped?
With .NET 8+ Blazor United (interactive server + auto render modes), is there renewed demand for this?
Would the SKPicture serialization approach from BlazorSkiaSharp repo be viable for production use?

resolution.hypothesis: Server-side Blazor support requires a fundamentally different rendering pipeline that serializes drawing operations and sends them to the client for execution, since the server cannot directly access browser APIs.

proposals.title: Server-side image rendering workaround
proposals.description: Use SkiaSharp on the server to render to SKImage/SKBitmap, encode as PNG/JPEG, and serve as a data URI or via an endpoint. This works today without any library changes but provides no interactivity.

proposals.title: SKPicture serialization approach
proposals.description: Productize mattleibow's BlazorSkiaSharp prototype: serialize drawing commands as SKPicture on the server, send the binary over SignalR, deserialize and render on the WASM client. This provides interactive rendering but requires SkiaSharp loaded on both sides.

proposals.title: Use Blazor WASM or Auto render mode
proposals.description: For interactive SkiaSharp rendering in Blazor, use the WASM or Auto render mode (available in .NET 8+) where SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor already works. This avoids the server-side problem entirely.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Use Blazor WASM or Auto render mode
resolution.recommendedReason: With .NET 8+ Blazor Auto render mode, apps can start with server-side rendering and transition to WASM once downloaded, which addresses the original concern about download size. This uses existing SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor support without new development.

actionability.reason: Valid feature request with strong community interest (19 reactions, 48 comments). The WASM side was delivered but server-side rendering remains an open need. However, .NET 8 Blazor Auto render mode may reduce the urgency.

actions.description: Apply feature-request and Blazor views labels
Cross-reference PR #1811 which delivered the WASM half of this request
Cross-reference #1834 (Blazor Desktop support) which faces similar JSInterop limitations
Provide status update noting WASM support delivered, server-side still open, and suggest Blazor Auto render mode as alternative
actions.comment: Thanks for the detailed feature request and the extensive discussion here. The Blazor WebAssembly side of this was delivered in SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor (merged in PR #1811). However, server-side Blazor rendering remains unsupported — the current implementation requires browser-side JS interop and is marked `[SupportedOSPlatform("browser")]`. If you need interactive SkiaSharp rendering in Blazor today, the recommended path is to use Blazor WebAssembly or the Auto render mode available in .NET 8+. Auto mode starts with server-side rendering and transitions to WASM once the client assets are downloaded, which addresses the original concern about large client downloads. For server-only scenarios where interactivity isn't required, you can render with SkiaSharp on the server and serve the output as images via `` tags or data URIs. Keeping this open to track interest in native server-side Blazor rendering support.
